headline: Harbor Insurance is in active implementation crisis with declining adoption, a frustrated executive sponsor, and an unresolved SSO blocker threatening a $240K renewal in 39 days.
objectives: Establish a concrete, time-bound SSO resolution plan with clear owner and ETA to unblock the rollout
Rebuild executive confidence by demonstrating accountability and presenting a credible recovery timeline
Prevent further adoption decline by aligning on immediate interim steps users can take while SSO is resolved
topics_to_cover: SSO incident status update — current root cause, resolution owner, and hard ETA from engineering/support
Technical escalation outcome: confirm whether CSM-coordinated escalation from 2026-04-17 was executed and its current standing
Open ticket T001 review — walk through support findings on recurring admin SSO error and next steps
Rollout unblock plan — what can proceed independently of SSO, and what is gated on it
Active user decline (301 → 245 over 2 weeks) — acknowledge the trend and tie recovery plan to reversing it
Executive confidence — directly address sponsor's frustration, set expectations, and define a communication cadence through resolution
Renewal timeline awareness — renewal is 2026-06-12; recovery plan must be credible before that date
open_followups_from_last_call: CSM (Morgan Lee) was to coordinate a technical escalation on the SSO blocker following the 2026-04-17 call — confirm this was completed and provide status update to the customer
risks_to_flag: Renewal is 39 days out (2026-06-12) with a health score of 48 and declining — insufficient recovery time if SSO is not resolved within the next 1–2 weeks
Executive sponsor is actively frustrated (NPS: 2, T001 sentiment: frustrated) — loss of executive champion could accelerate churn decision
Active user base has dropped 19% in two weeks (301 → 245); if trend continues, organizational momentum for the platform may be lost before rollout completes
9 support tickets in 30 days signals systemic trust erosion, not just a single incident — broader implementation quality perception risk
Low expansion signal combined with current trajectory suggests upsell conversation is off the table until stability is demonstrated
questions_to_ask_customer: Since the SSO issues began, how has the experience affected your internal team's confidence in the platform — and what would a successful resolution need to look like for that confidence to be restored before renewal?
Beyond SSO, are there other implementation blockers or frustrations your team is encountering that haven't made it into a support ticket yet?
What does your executive sponsor need to see from us in the next two weeks to remain committed to the rollout going forward?
Which parts of the rollout could your team realistically move forward with today, even while SSO is being resolved, and what internal resources would that require?

headline: Northstar Travel is entering renewal in 15 days with a declining health score (52), a broken mobile onboarding flow blocking their core field-team adoption initiative, and an NPS of 3 — making this call critical for both retention and trust recovery.
objectives: Confirm T003 resolution timeline and unblock mobile onboarding for field team users before renewal date (2026-05-20)
Reassure the customer of product commitment and de-risk the renewal conversation by demonstrating accountability
Align on a concrete mobile adoption plan with measurable milestones the customer can bring back internally
topics_to_cover: Status update on T003 (mobile onboarding bug) — severity, ETA, and interim workaround if fix is not yet deployed
Impact assessment: how many field-team users are blocked and what business workflows are affected
Mobile onboarding plan delivery (follow-up item from 2026-04-16 call — confirm receipt and walk through it)
Usage trend discussion: active users down from 99 to 87 in two weeks; align on root cause and recovery plan
Renewal timeline: contract expires 2026-05-20; address any internal blockers or approvals needed on their side
NPS of 3 — acknowledge directly and ask what would need to change to improve their experience
open_followups_from_last_call: Mobile onboarding plan was to be sent to Northstar Travel after the 2026-04-16 call — confirm it was delivered and whether the customer has reviewed it
risks_to_flag: Renewal is in 15 days (2026-05-20) while a medium-severity bug is actively blocking the mobile onboarding flow that was the stated goal of the engagement — this creates a direct churn risk
Health score has dropped 19 points and is now at 52; combined with NPS of 3, the customer sentiment is significantly negative entering renewal
Active user count has declined 12% in two weeks (99 → 87); power users also dropped from 37 to 30, suggesting disengagement beyond the bug itself
4 support tickets in 30 days with negative sentiment signals the customer may feel under-supported; escalation or executive involvement may be warranted if T003 does not have a clear fix date
questions_to_ask_customer: Since the mobile onboarding issue was reported, how has it affected your field team's day-to-day workflows, and have there been any internal escalations on your side because of it?
When you think about what would need to be true for you to feel confident renewing on May 20th, what are the one or two things that matter most right now?
After reviewing the mobile onboarding plan we sent over, what parts of it feel realistic for your team to execute, and where do you anticipate the biggest friction?
Beyond the current bug, are there other areas where the platform isn't meeting your team's expectations that we haven't addressed yet?

headline: Cobalt Health is on a strong growth trajectory with weekly active users up 21% over the past two weeks and a high-signal expansion opportunity underway as the analytics team prepares to onboard onto the platform.
objectives: Align on a concrete rollout timeline and ownership structure for the analytics department expansion
Deliver or walk through best-practice configuration guidance to unblock T012 and set the new department up for success
Confirm next steps and assign a follow-up owner for the rollout plan promised on the 2026-04-25 call
topics_to_cover: Status of the analytics team rollout: scope, headcount, and target go-live date
Best-practice setup for new department onboarding (directly addresses T012)
Review of the rollout plan shared (or to be shared) as follow-up from the April 25 call
Usage growth context: active users up from 243 to 294 in two weeks — celebrate the momentum
Potential expansion SKU or seat-count adjustment needed to support analytics team
Training and enablement resources for the new department
open_followups_from_last_call: Share rollout plan with Cobalt Health (committed on 2026-04-25 expansion discussion call — confirm whether this was sent and review together)
risks_to_flag: Best-practice configuration for the new department has not yet been established — delayed guidance could slow the rollout and dampen adoption in the analytics team
Rollout plan follow-up from April 25 call must be confirmed as delivered; if not yet shared, this is a credibility risk given a 10-day gap
Rapid user growth (21% in two weeks) may surface capacity, permission, or governance questions that need proactive addressing before the analytics team joins
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: Cobalt Health has an NPS of 9 and a health score trending upward to 88 — this is an ideal moment to discuss a formal expansion of seats or tier to accommodate the analytics department, and to explore whether the primary champion would be willing to serve as a reference or case study given the strong adoption curve.
questions_to_ask_customer: What does success look like for the analytics team at the 30- and 90-day marks post-rollout, and who internally owns that outcome?
Are there any specific workflows or data sources the analytics team needs to connect to that you haven't yet been able to configure — and would those be blockers to go-live?
Given how quickly your active user count has grown, are there governance or access-control requirements from IT or compliance we should factor into the new department's setup?
What would make you confident enough in the rollout to expand this to additional departments beyond analytics later this year?

headline: Futura Robotics is showing strong and accelerating adoption (77→98 active users in two weeks) with high expansion signals around premium automation, but budget approval remains an open gate.
objectives: Confirm whether the premium automation options sent post-4/22 call were reviewed and get a clear read on budget approval status
Walk through automation feature limits and relevant premium use cases so the champion can build an internal business case
Identify the decision-maker and timeline needed to move an expansion conversation forward before the September renewal
topics_to_cover: Follow-up on premium automation options document sent after 2026-04-22 call — received and reviewed?
Current automation limits on their plan and where Futura Robotics is hitting or approaching those limits
Premium automation use cases most relevant to their robotics workflows and power-user cohort (now 54 users)
Open ticket T011: address questions about premium automation feature limits directly
Budget approval process and internal stakeholders involved
Renewal timeline (2026-09-30) and how an expansion could be structured alongside it
open_followups_from_last_call: Send premium automation options — confirm receipt and whether the team has had a chance to review (action was on Riley post-4/22 call)
Budget approval was flagged as a risk — status and next steps from the customer's side?
risks_to_flag: Budget approval dependency could stall an expansion decision; if not engaged soon, the renewal window (Sept 30) may compress the timeline uncomfortably

Rapid user growth (98 active, 54 power-users) may mean the team is already bumping against plan limits without a formal upgrade path in place, creating a friction or churn risk if not addressed proactively
T011 is still in 'new' status — ensure the automation limits question is resolved on this call to prevent sentiment from shifting
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: With 54 power-users and a growing active base, Futura Robotics is a strong candidate for a premium automation tier upgrade ahead of the September renewal. Framing the expansion as unlocking capacity for the power-user cohort — with an ROI angle tied to automation efficiency in their robotics workflows — gives the champion a concrete business case for internal budget approval. If the expansion closes successfully, they would also be a compelling case study or reference for industrial/robotics mid-market prospects.
questions_to_ask_customer: Since we sent over the premium automation options after our last call, have you had a chance to share those internally — and what was the reaction from stakeholders?
When you look at how your power-users are working today, which specific automation workflows are they running most heavily, and where do they feel the most constrained?
What does the budget approval process look like on your end, and who else needs to be part of the conversation before a decision can move forward?
Given your team's growth trajectory, what would need to be true for you to feel confident recommending a premium tier upgrade before your renewal in September?

headline: Juniper Manufacturing is entering this call with three consecutive weeks of declining active users (104→87) and a trust issue with report exports that remains unresolved from the prior session, creating meaningful renewal risk at 57 days out.
objectives: Confirm root cause of the reporting module usage drop and present a clear remediation path or timeline
Rebuild confidence in export data accuracy by addressing the data freshness issue identified on 2026-04-24
Stabilize the account relationship and agree on a recovery milestone plan ahead of the 2026-07-02 renewal
topics_to_cover: Status update on T004: power user interpretation of dashboard drop in reporting module
Data freshness investigation outcome — what was found, what is being fixed, and by when
Export data trust: walk through current data pipeline accuracy and any known discrepancies
Usage trend review: active users dropped from 104 to 87 over three weeks — identify which departments are affected and why
Confirm which two departments have declining usage and whether it is adoption, workflow, or product issue
Outline a concrete recovery plan with measurable checkpoints before renewal
open_followups_from_last_call: Investigate data freshness issue flagged on 2026-04-24 — status must be reported this call
risks_to_flag: Renewal is 57 days out (2026-07-02) with health score at 58 and declining — window to recover is narrow
NPS of 5 and 6 support tickets in 30 days indicate growing dissatisfaction that could accelerate churn risk
Unresolved export data trust issue may be actively suppressing adoption; power users avoiding the reporting module compounds the usage decline
Power user disengagement (38→27 in two weeks) is disproportionately dangerous — these users typically drive broader team adoption
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: Expansion signal is rated medium, but it should not be pursued until the usage and trust issues are resolved. If the data freshness fix lands well and usage stabilizes, a conversation about expanding reporting capabilities or additional department seats could be reopened in June as a renewal sweetener.
questions_to_ask_customer: Since our last call, have your power users changed how they're using — or avoiding — the reporting module, and what specific concerns are they raising about the export data?
Which two departments are seeing the steepest drop in usage, and is that being driven by a workflow change on your side or frustration with the product?
What would 'trusted data' look like to your team — is there a specific report or export they need to validate before they'll feel confident using the dashboard again?
As we approach your July renewal, what does success with this platform need to look like over the next 60 days for your stakeholders to feel good about continuing?

headline: Quartz Media is in critical churn territory with usage in freefall, no identified champion, an NPS of 2, and a high-severity ticket stating the team has stopped using the platform — this call is a last meaningful intervention before the July renewal.
objectives: Identify and confirm a named internal sponsor or champion who will own platform adoption going forward
Align on a concrete, time-boxed reactivation plan with specific milestones before the July 22 renewal
Uncover and acknowledge the root causes of disengagement to rebuild trust and demonstrate responsiveness
topics_to_cover: Acknowledge the team's disengagement openly and validate their frustration — do not minimize
Review the decline in active and power users (21 → 16 active, 7 → 3 power users over 3 weeks)
Champion gap: who is currently accountable for the platform internally, and who should be
Status of high-severity ticket T006 and what a 'reset' means to them concretely
Platform value re-anchoring: revisit original use case and whether it still applies
Proposed reactivation structure: owner, success criteria, timeline, and check-in cadence
Upcoming renewal on 2026-07-22 and what needs to be true for them to renew
open_followups_from_last_call: Identify a new internal sponsor to replace the missing champion — unresolved as of 2026-04-09
Propose a formal platform reset plan — flagged on 2026-04-09, now escalated via T006 but still without a structured response
risks_to_flag: Churn risk is high: health score at 46 and declining, NPS of 2, and customer's own team reports they have stopped using the platform
No identified champion or internal owner creates a vacuum — even a good reset plan may fail without one
Renewal is only 76 days away; insufficient time for a full recovery cycle if reactivation is delayed further
28-day gap since last contact (2026-04-09 to 2026-05-07) during a high-severity open ticket signals responsiveness risk to the customer
Power user collapse (7 → 3) suggests the most engaged individuals have disengaged, which is harder to reverse than general low adoption
questions_to_ask_customer: When your team stopped using the platform, what was the moment or trigger that caused that shift — was it a specific experience, a change in your team, or something else?
Who in your organization today feels most accountable for making your investment in this platform succeed, and do they have the capacity to lead a reset?
If we were to design a reset from scratch together, what would 'working well' look like for your team in 60 days — what does success actually mean to you?
Given where things stand, what would need to be true over the next few weeks for you to feel confident going into your July renewal?

headline: Orchid Telecom enters this integration roadmap review with an unresolved product roadmap dependency blocking their next rollout, a flat health score of 79, and a passive NPS of 6 — despite high expansion signals.
objectives: Deliver a concrete update on the integration roadmap timeline from the product escalation raised on 2026-04-25
Clarify the specific dependency blocking Orchid Telecom's next rollout and jointly define a mitigation or workaround plan
Strengthen the strategic relationship by acknowledging the account's importance and aligning on a shared path forward toward renewal and expansion
topics_to_cover: Status update on integration roadmap escalation to product (follow-up from 2026-04-25 call)
T009 resolution: platform team's outstanding request for roadmap visibility
Nature and timeline of the rollout dependency — what is blocked and when does it become critical
Workaround or interim options if roadmap delivery is delayed
Usage trend discussion: active users holding flat at ~265; opportunity to drive deeper adoption among non-power users
Strategic relationship health: NPS 6 is passive — surface any underlying dissatisfaction
Expansion opportunity: high expansion signal noted — explore appetite if roadmap concerns are resolved
open_followups_from_last_call: Escalate integration roadmap question to product (raised 2026-04-25) — T009 remains open; bring a response to this call
risks_to_flag: Roadmap dependency is blocking a customer rollout — prolonged ambiguity could jeopardize the 2026-11-01 renewal if the rollout slips significantly
NPS of 6 (passive) at a strategic $300K account indicates latent dissatisfaction that could harden if the roadmap issue is not resolved promptly
Flat usage trend with no growth over the past three weeks may signal that adoption is stalled pending the integration deliverable
If the product escalation from 2026-04-25 does not yet have a concrete answer, arriving to this call without one will damage credibility — flag internally before the call
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: Expansion signal is rated high, making this account a strong candidate for upsell once the roadmap dependency is resolved. Use this call to plant the seed: frame resolution of the integration issue as the enabler for the broader rollout, and tie expanded rollout scope to additional product capabilities. If the customer expresses satisfaction with how the roadmap concern is handled, this is also a moment to explore reference or case study participation ahead of renewal.
questions_to_ask_customer: Can you walk us through exactly where the integration dependency sits in your rollout plan — what milestone is gated on it, and what's your hard deadline before it impacts the business?
Beyond the integration roadmap, what would need to be true over the next 90 days for you to feel genuinely confident heading into your November renewal?
Your active user count has been steady around 265 — are there specific teams or use cases you've been holding back from onboarding until the integration is in place?
If we're unable to hit the exact roadmap timeline you need, what interim solution or level of visibility from our product team would give your platform team enough to move forward?

headline: Lumen Energy enters this call with persistent permissions confusion and 8 support tickets in 30 days, but strong underlying usage signals an enablement gap rather than a product or churn risk ahead of an August renewal.
objectives: Complete or schedule structured admin training to address the permissions model confusion surfaced in T005 and the April 23 call
Deliver and walk through the permissions guide committed on the last call to close that open follow-up
Assess whether resolving permissions friction can convert the medium expansion signal into a concrete upsell conversation before the August renewal
topics_to_cover: Status check on permissions confusion — have admins encountered new blockers since April 23?
Deliver and review the permissions guide (committed follow-up from last call)
Walk through advanced permissions concepts tied to T005 how-to questions
Admin training: confirm format, attendees, and schedule a date
Ticket volume (8 in 30 days) — identify if there is a pattern beyond permissions
Health score context (63, flat) and NPS of 4 — acknowledge and probe sentiment
Renewal timeline awareness (August 5) and any concerns the team has entering that window
open_followups_from_last_call: Provide permissions guide — committed on 2026-04-23 call; delivery status must be confirmed at the top of this call
risks_to_flag: NPS of 4 is a churn-adjacent signal; unresolved admin friction is likely suppressing score and must be addressed before renewal in August
8 support tickets in 30 days with a flat health score (63) suggests confusion is not self-resolving and may escalate without structured enablement
Renewal is only ~90 days out — if permissions issues persist, they could become a negotiating obstacle or reason to downsize
Permissions guide not yet confirmed as delivered — if still outstanding, customer trust and follow-through credibility are at risk
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: Usage remains strong and stable (~260 actives, ~116 power-users weekly), indicating the team finds real value in the product. If permissions are unblocked through training, power-user adoption could grow, supporting a seat or tier expansion conversation ahead of the August renewal.
questions_to_ask_customer: Since our last call, what specific permissions scenarios are still causing your admins the most friction day-to-day?
Who are the key admins we should include in a formal training session, and what would make that session most useful for your team?
Beyond permissions, are there other areas driving the support tickets your team has been opening — and is there anything systemic we should be solving together?
As you look toward your August renewal, what would need to be true about the admin experience for you to feel fully confident in the platform?

headline: Redwood Cloud is entering an expansion rollout design call with strong usage momentum (+19% active users in two weeks) and high expansion signals, but no governance plan yet finalized to support multi-team adoption.
objectives: Align on a concrete governance framework (ownership, permissions, onboarding standards) before expanding to additional teams
Co-design a phased rollout approach for multi-team adoption, including sequencing and success milestones
Identify internal champion(s) and any blockers that could stall the expansion ahead of the October renewal
topics_to_cover: Review of the draft phased rollout approach promised on 2026-04-26
Governance plan: team ownership model, admin roles, data/access policies across teams
Multi-team adoption sequencing — which teams are next and readiness criteria
Power-user growth trends and how to leverage the 162 power users as internal advocates
Success metrics for the expansion phase (adoption targets, activation thresholds)
Timeline alignment relative to the 2026-10-30 renewal
open_followups_from_last_call: Draft phased rollout approach (committed 2026-04-26) — confirm it has been prepared and walk customer through it
risks_to_flag: Governance plan is still undefined; expanding to new teams without it risks inconsistent adoption, permission conflicts, or shadow usage that is hard to manage later
Rapid user growth (244 → 290 actives in two weeks) without a structured onboarding plan could dilute the power-user ratio and reduce perceived value in new teams
No named executive sponsor or cross-team project owner mentioned — single-team dependency could slow multi-team rollout
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: With an NPS of 9 and a visibly engaged platform team, this is a strong moment to identify a named internal champion who can co-present the expansion business case internally and, downstream, serve as a reference or case study. The usage growth curve is a compelling data point to anchor an expansion conversation and potentially upsell additional seats or a higher-tier plan ahead of the October renewal.
questions_to_ask_customer: Which teams are you planning to onboard in the first wave, and what does 'ready to expand' look like from your perspective for each of them?
Who will own governance centrally — is there a platform or IT stakeholder already mapped to this, or is that still being decided?
Looking at your 162 power users today, how are you thinking about activating them as internal enablers when new teams come on board?
What would need to be true by your October renewal for this expansion to be considered a clear success internally?

headline: Acme Retail is entering a critical pre-renewal window with a sharply declining health score (81→72), a 23% drop in active users over three weeks, and an unresolved dashboard export data issue fueling customer frustration.
objectives: Confirm status and timeline for resolving T010 (missing dashboard export data) and restore customer confidence in the reporting module
Understand root causes of the adoption decline and agree on a concrete re-engagement plan with named owners and milestones
Gauge renewal sentiment and identify any additional blockers or unmet needs before the 2026-07-15 renewal date
topics_to_cover: Status update on T010: dashboard exports missing recent data — what has been investigated, what is the resolution ETA
Active user and power-user decline trend (106→82 active, 40→25 power users in three weeks) — causes and impact on their team
Reporting module adoption: which teams or use cases have pulled back and why
Agreed remediation steps with clear owners and deadlines on both sides
Renewal timeline awareness — position next steps as part of a mutual success plan ahead of July 15
open_followups_from_last_call: Investigate reporting data issue flagged on 2026-04-21 call — T010 remains open as of 2026-04-24 with no resolution noted
risks_to_flag: Health score declined 9 points in under a month; trajectory points to further deterioration if adoption is not stabilized before renewal
T010 is open and unresolved 17+ days after the prior call; continued delay risks deepening frustration (customer sentiment already flagged as frustrated)
Power-user base dropped 37.5% (40→25) in three weeks — loss of internal champions weakens renewal case and increases churn risk
NPS of 6 and low expansion signal indicate limited goodwill buffer heading into renewal negotiations
Only 65 days to renewal (2026-07-15); insufficient time for a slow recovery if action is not taken immediately
questions_to_ask_customer: Since the dashboard export issue started, which specific reporting workflows or decisions have been blocked or delayed for your team?
Beyond the export bug, what other factors have contributed to fewer people using the reporting module over the past few weeks?
Who internally owns the reporting use case now, and what would need to be true for them to feel confident recommending renewal to leadership?
If we resolve the export issue and put a re-engagement plan in place by end of May, what does a successful outcome look like for you by renewal time?

headline: BrightPath Logistics enters this call in a fragile state—health score declining, a high-severity sync failure ticket still open with no root cause identified, and renewal just 49 days away.
objectives: Deliver a concrete root cause or interim mitigation plan for the recurring sync failures (T002)
Rebuild customer confidence by demonstrating accountability and a clear path to stability before the June 30 renewal
Align on a mutual success plan with defined milestones to arrest the health score decline
topics_to_cover: Status update on T002: three sync failures this week, logs obtained but root cause still pending
Technical escalation path: who owns resolution, timeline, and next engineering touchpoint
Impact assessment: how sync failures are affecting BrightPath's operations and end users
Usage trend review: slight dip from 265 to 257 active users—confirm whether related to instability
Renewal readiness: acknowledge the June 30 date and set expectations for stabilization timeline
Agree on a cadence for proactive status updates between now and resolution
open_followups_from_last_call: Escalate integration logs to engineering for root cause analysis (committed 2026-04-18, still unresolved as of T002 on 2026-04-29)
risks_to_flag: Renewal is in 49 days with health score at 61 and declining—unresolved technical issues could block renewal or trigger downsell
NPS of 4 signals significant dissatisfaction; customer sentiment on T002 is flagged as 'concerned'
No root cause identified after repeated sync failures erodes trust in the platform's reliability
24-day gap since last contact (2026-04-18 to 2026-05-12) during an active high-severity incident may have compounded frustration
Flat usage trend with a slight active-user dip could accelerate if instability continues
expansion_or_advocacy_angle: Expansion signal is rated medium, but any advocacy or upsell conversation must be deferred until sync stability is fully resolved and customer confidence is restored. Once stabilized, the consistent power-user base (~117) could be a foundation for a workflow automation or advanced integration tier conversation ahead of renewal.
questions_to_ask_customer: Since the sync failures started, what has the downstream operational impact been for your team—are there specific workflows or shipments that have been disrupted?
When you think about what a fully stable integration looks like over the next 30 days, what would need to be true for you to feel confident heading into renewal?
Beyond the sync issue, are there other areas of the platform that aren't meeting your expectations right now that we haven't yet discussed?
Who on your side is most affected by these failures, and would it be valuable to include your technical lead in a follow-up call with our engineering team to walk through the logs together?
